inline_comments.message: Only use abbreviations when when they are commonly known, and "nd" is not.  Take the time to spell out "network-discovery" and everyone will know what you're talking about.
This shouldn't need to be configured.  Some can be discovered from environment (port, hostname); others like the path shouldn't be changed or things will break.
@Autowired is redundant with @Value
The REST framework will do the unmarshalling for you - just specify the type you want the JSON converted to.
Imports are not in the correct order
If these are truly unused, remove them.  If they are used, remove the annotation
You are the callback not the client - this should be renamed.
Do this conversion in the configuration bean so it's done just once and not on every call.
Why V suffix?  If unit is milliseconds, timeoutMs would make more sense.
typo remainding => remaining
Iterating over a list that can be mutated by other threads is not thread-safe.
Vservers can also be associated directly with VNF, like in vFW demo.  You will need to check those as well, not just VNFCs.
Use a real REST client to make REST calls.
Handle/log exceptions correctly
new String?  Really?
Parameters must be URL-encoded.  But you shouldn't be building URLs yourself and instead use a real REST client to do this.
You don't need to do this unmarshalling manually.  Just add NetworkDiscoveryResponse ndResponse as a method parameter instead of InputStream is.

networkDiscoveryList can't be an instance variable - what if there are two concurrent context builder calls?

You need a map of requestId => networkDiscoveryInfoList

This will throw an exception because you're trying to update an immutable list (created by Arrays.asList)

I don't see an concurrency handling - the callback will be called concurrently by Network Discovery service.
A better name would be /networkDiscoveryNotification since you're receiving a notification from Network Discovery, not performing any query.
The callback consumes NetworkDiscoveryResponse, not returning it.  Network Discovery service doesn't really look at callback output - you can just return text/plain "Acknowledged"
Fix copy paste issues
You shouldn't be returning network discovery response - you are supposed to consume it.
Where did this come from?  You should use existing class like java.util.concurrent.CountDownLatch instead.
Should we include the ip address as part of the check in?
Is this always going to be localhost?

I don't quite understand this barrier business. Perhaps include some comments.

If this is just a simple timeout mechanism, isn't it a bit over complicated?
Generic Exception?
Need proper exception handling here.
use /* comment */
Is it TODO for the following method?  If so, it makes more sense to add this inside the method.
use /* comment */
Should this be removed?
why are we changing the requestId?  If it's a requirement, please add a comment.
Should probably have a constant for "GET" etc.
Need proper exception handling here.
same comment as above
same comment as above
should be able to combine responseList == null and responseList.isEmpty

This method works and tested. It is also consistent with the similar method to "ServiceDecompositionMicroService" in the same class. Will update the method to the preferred client type when enhancing the class.

I tested with the suggested way, but got an error at running time, complaining the NetworkDiscoveryResponse contains nested unusual type "Resources":

Unrecognized field "resources" (class org.onap.sdnc.apps.pomba.networkdiscovery.datamodel.NetworkDiscoveryResponse), not marked as ignorable (4 known properties: "requestId", "code", "message", "ackFinalIndicator"])
 at [Source: org.glassfish.jersey.message.internal.ReaderInterceptorExecutor$UnCloseableInputStream@19e669d5; line: 1, column: 87] (through reference chain: org.onap.sdnc.apps.pomba.networkdiscovery.datamodel.NetworkDiscoveryResponse["resources"])

The concurrency is now handled by SignalBarrier in the calling function.
I also updated networkDiscoveryInfoList to be static.

The concurrency is handled by Signal Barrier in the message sending method.

noted
Please merge all changes made by Trevor - none of the ajsc stuff should be here.
The correct thing then is to address the problem, not work around it.  You should have mentioned this to me.
An instance variable to handle concurrency is not acceptable.  How are you going to handle multiple context builder calls for different service instance ids with a single lock?
Barrier is not the correct idiom to wait on multiple threads.  Change this to CountDownLatch, as per previous comment.
Done
for consistency with the above, rename "nd" to "networkDiscovery"

If you want to configure the context builder port, you'd change the spring parameter, not this one - use the existing parameter instead of creating your own for the callback URL.

Likewise, hard-code http for callback until https is supported, then hard-code that.
This file is AJSC-specific and should have been deleted

whitespace issues

Change your eclipse settings to not use tabs.  Use the AnyEdit plugin to automatically fix whitespace.
whitespace issues
Change to resourceId - may a network ID, or vnfc ID, etc.
Fix vServerId

Change "is" (from when type was InputStream) to something more appropriate, e.g. "notification"

Also, whitespace issues.

Need to use lock when accessing networkDiscoveryInfoList.

In fact, remove the entry from the map here so you don't have to do it below (430-437)
What about the l3-networks?
vserverId => resourceId
The URL should not include the query parameters - those are added below by the builder.  This should be the base URL.
Don't create new configurations!  The client should just be an @Autowired bean and can be reused for every request.
These must be query parameters, not headers
This method is not needed, and doesn't encode URLs correctly anyway.
whitespace issues
"is" -> "notification"
Do the authorization verification (which is missing) here in the REST layer so you don't need to pass authorization to the serviceImpl.
Ack or OK is fine, don't need both...

Network discovery request is not just for vserver, it can be any resource type.  This should be replaced with

 String resourceType; // e.g. vserver
 String resourceId;   // e.g. vserverId
Initial this with new ArrayList<>() and you don't need null check when using it.
"is" was variable name when type was InputStream.  Should be renamed to something more appropriate, e.g. notification

Don't use Arrays.asList(), as it creates an unmodifiable List.  That will cause exception on line 398 when you try to add to it.

Just initialize list in RspInfo class.  You will still need to add concurrency support if you use ArrayList which is not thread-safe.
This is the wrong place to add to the map - the rspInfo must already be in place otherwise this method doesn't work.

Whitespace issues.
Don't eat the exception and return null - throw the exception instead
Whitespace issues
Comments are backwards
This is not correct import order - see coding guidelines
This should be done in RestServiceImpl - auth is part of the HTTP stack.
Why are you updating the map here?
Unlock immediately after get on line 393 - there should be minimal code protected by the lock

Replace get on line 433 with remove and unlock immediately

Always use try/finally when locking and unlocking
Delete this - query parameters are added by client below

Replace with this - property has nothing to do with REST or HTTP

            Response response = jerseyClient.target(networkDiscoveryBaseUrl)
                    .queryParam(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_REQUEST_ID, requestId)
                    .queryParam(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_RESOURCE_TYPE, resourceType)
                    .queryParam(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_RESOURCE_ID, resourceId)
                    .queryParam(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_NOTIFICATION_URL, callbackUrlStr)
                    .request()
                    .header(HttpHeaders.CONTENT_TYPE, "application/json")
                    .header(HttpHeaders.ACCEPT, "application/json")
                    .header(HttpHeaders.AUTHORIZATION, getNdBasicAuthorization())
                    .header(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_X_ONAP_PARTNER_NAME, partner_name)
                    .header(NETWORK_DISCOVERY_FIND_RESOURCE_BY_TYPE_REST_X_ONAP_REQUEST_ID, parent_requestId)
                    .get();
Delete this - query parameters are handled by REST client and you're not encoding URL safely anyway
Remove or add correct MediaType (TEXT_PLAIN is fine)
Authorization should return 401 not 500
We need separate basic auth credentials for the microservice.
Need basic auth here as well.
The JerseyConfiguration already gives you this.
Don't reuse the Basic Auth that was passed in from the Context Aggregator, configure this authoriztion separately.

> Unless an explicit copy of {@code original} is needed, use of this constructor is unnecessary since Strings are immutable.
typo

It appears these are used only for junit, but as public methods they can be called by anyone for any reason.  Consider changing these to package-protected and move test to same package (under src/test/java).
